chunk_summary: This section outlines a structured tele-triage script and pathway-specific intake fields for tele-nutrition workflows, emphasizing auditable processes, equity, and digital tools for risk stratification and scheduling.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of structured tele-triage scripts in tele-nutrition workflows.
Learn pathway-specific intake data fields for conditions like malnutrition, obesity, anemia, and chronic diseases.
Explore equity-focused strategies for caregiver engagement in tele-nutrition services.
key_facts: Structured intake reduces unsafe variability and ensures auditable tele-nutrition workflows.
Urgent clinician review is flagged if symptoms like difficulty breathing, seizures, or severe diarrhea are reported.
Pathway-specific intake fields include MUAC for malnutrition (age 6–59 months), hemoglobin values for anemia, and beverage/snack patterns for obesity.
Equity rule: Offer scripts in the caregiver’s preferred language and allow audio completion for accessibility.
Digital forms auto-populate telenotes, assign risk tiers, and schedule follow-ups efficiently.
reference_quote: Structured intake reduces unsafe variability and makes team-delivered tele-nutrition auditable. (Source: Telemedicine Practice Guidelines, Reference 1)
